---
title: 'catalog add'
---

## Synopsis

```bash
xorq catalog [OPTIONS] add [--sync/--no-sync] [-a ALIAS]... PATHS...
```

## Description

Add one or more build entries to the catalog from archive files (`.zip`) or build directories.

Each path is inspected and handled accordingly:

- **File** (`.zip`) — added directly as a catalog entry
- **Directory** — zipped and then added

The entry name is derived from the archive content. If an entry with the same name already exists, the command fails.

When `--sync` is enabled (the default), the catalog pulls from remotes before adding and pushes after, keeping the catalog synchronized.

## Options

`--sync / --no-sync`
: Synchronize with remotes before and after adding. Default: `--sync`.

`-a, --alias ALIAS`
: Create an alias for the added entry. Can be repeated to create multiple aliases.

## Examples

```bash
# Add a single archive
xorq catalog --name my-catalog add build.zip

# Add with aliases
xorq catalog --name my-catalog add build.zip -a latest -a v2.1

# Add a build directory
xorq catalog --name my-catalog add /tmp/my-build/

# Add multiple archives without syncing
xorq catalog --name my-catalog add --no-sync a.zip b.zip
```

## See also

- [catalog list](ls.qmd)
- [catalog rm](rm.qmd)
